title: podman login to private secure registry keeps getting Error 404 not founds.  Works fine with Docker. /kindbug
body:
**Description**

I'm trying to migrate to podman from docker on my Red Hat 7.7 systems.  The problem I've run into so far is that I cannot log into my private secure registry that's running on Nexus 3.19.1.  I keep getting "Error 404" not found.  Everything works fine with docker of course since that's been running for months now.  I've got to get this working or this will be a no go for me not being able to share images that I have in my current repository.  I've had a very difficult time trying to find information about supporting private secure registries with podman  (only insecure), so this is my last recourse.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. sudo podman login -u myusername -p  my password myprivaterepo

**Describe the results you received:**

Error: error authenticating creds for "myprivaterepo": pinging docker registry returned: error pinging registry myprivaterepo, response code 404 (Not Found)


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

I'm using a self-signed cert that's been run through update-ca-trust.  I've also tried pointing the command to look at the cert (--cert-dir) to see if that was the problem, but I get the same results.  I also cleared the authentication token found in the docker config.json file as well.


**Output of `podman version`:**
Stock Redhat 7.7 rpm.
```
Version:            1.4.4
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.10.3
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
⋊
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-1.4.4-4.el7.x86_64
```

Physical baremetal servers.

comments.body:
An HTTP-level result like a 404 error would only be possible after encryption for the connection was successfully set up, so if you're using TLS, your certificate setup looks fine.

Can you re-run the login attempt using `--log-level=debug` and paste the details?  Just to be sure, are you pointing your client at the repository's HTTP or HTTPS connector ports?

The registry is running on port 443 (HTTPS) via nginx (other services running on the system).   Maybe the proxy is interfering with it for some reason? There's a special http_user_agent setting for docker access that redirects behind 443.

Here's the dump trying to connect the way I do and add :443 to the servername.

```
sudo podman login --log-level=debug -u myusername -p mypassword myprivaterepo                            DEBU[0000] Looking for TLS certificates and private keys in /etc/docker/certs.d/myprivaterepo
DEBU[0000] GET https://myprivaterepo/v2/ 
DEBU[0000] Ping https://myprivaterepo/v2/ status 404 
DEBU[0000] GET https://myprivaterepo/v1/_ping 
DEBU[0000] Ping https://myprivaterepo/v1/_ping status 404 
ERRO[0000] error authenticating creds for "myprivaterepo": pinging docker registry returned: error pinging registry myprivaterepo, response code 404 (Not Found) 
```

Adding :443

```
sudo podman login --log-level=debug -u myusername -p mypassword myprivaterepo:443                                                                                   DEBU[0000] Looking for TLS certificates and private keys in /etc/docker/certs.d/myprivaterepo:443
DEBU[0000]  crt: /etc/docker/certs.d/myprivaterepo:443/ca.crt
DEBU[0000] GET https://myprivaterepo:443/v2/
DEBU[0000] Ping https://myprivaterepo:443/v2/ status 404
DEBU[0000] GET https://myprivaterepo:443/v1/_ping
DEBU[0000] Ping https://myprivaterepo:443/v1/_ping status 404
ERRO[0000] error authenticating creds for "myprivaterepo:443": pinging docker registry returned: error pinging registry myprivaterepo:443, response code 404 (Not Found)
```
Got it!  I added to my nginx-proxy configuration a http_user_agent "Go-http-client"  to route it to the registry behind the proxy and I got "Login Succeeded!"
I had to use wireshark to capture the http session and disable tls-verify to turn off encryption to see what user agent podman was using.
